# Kimura Grip Mechanics from Closed Guard

The kimura, also called the double wristlock, is a shoulder lock attacked from
many positions. From closed guard it begins with wrist control on the opponent's
posting arm.

## Breaking the Grip

When the opponent answers the phone to defend the kimura, the critical mechanic
is driving your elbow toward their far hip rather than trying to muscle the arm
backward. Muscling the arm fights their entire body; the elbow drive isolates the
shoulder joint and removes their leverage.

Establish the figure-four grip first. Your near hand grips their wrist, your far
hand threads under their arm and grips your own wrist. This is the control frame.

## Finishing

Rotate their forearm toward the back of their head while keeping your elbows
tight. Hip escape slightly to create the angle. The finish is slow and positional,
never a jerk.
